mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the unemployment rate signals improving labor market conditions, which supports wage growth and consumer spending, driving corporate revenues and leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If a decline in UNRATE is followed by negative 20-day SPX returns in more than 50% of instances over a 3-year rolling window, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises above 0.5% after a prolonged period near zero, it signals the start of a tightening cycle that increases discount rates and depresses equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
regime_hint: first tightening after zero lower bound
failure_condition: If after the trigger condition, the 60-day forward SPX return is positive in more than 40% of cases over the next 5 years, the hypothesis fails.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increases in nonfarm payrolls and retail sales signal synchronized strength in labor markets and consumer spending, which is a powerful indicator of near-term corporate earnings growth and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the joint trigger condition occurs and 20-day forward SPX return is negative in more than 50% of cases, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: In a low inflation environment (CPIAUCSL year-over-year below 2.5%), an increase in industrial production signals robust economic activity without inflationary overheating, supporting equity valuations. In a high inflation environment, the same increase would signal tightening fears and depress returns, but given the current regime, the signal is positive.
failure_condition: If in the low inflation regime, a one-month increase in INDPRO leads to negative 60-day SPX returns more than 50% of the time, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A persistent increase in retail and food services sales (RSAFS) signals robust consumer demand, which directly drives corporate revenues and earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If month-over-month increase in RSAFS is followed by negative 20-day SPX returns in more than 50% of cases over a 3-year rolling window, the hypothesis fails.

mechanism_narrative: When the year-over-year change in industrial production exceeds 3%, it signals robust industrial activity and capacity utilization that boosts corporate earnings and investment, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If triggered instances yield negative 20-day SPX returns more than 40% of the time over a 3-year period, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts increase month-over-month while the federal funds rate remains below 1%, it indicates that monetary policy is highly accommodative and housing investment is accelerating, creating positive spillovers to the broader economy and equity market over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If the joint trigger condition occurs and 60-day forward SPX return is negative in more than 50% of cases, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is at or near zero (below 0.5%), the slope of the Treasury yield curve steepens as long-term rates rise on growth expectations, compressing term premiums and boosting equity valuations over a short horizon as investors rotate into stocks. The mechanism is active only while the funds rate remains below 0.5%.
failure_condition: If during the trigger condition (FEDFUNDS < 0.5%) the 5-day forward SPX return is negative more than 60% of the time over a 2-year period, the hypothesis is rejected.
